selected_important_turn.text: I know how that goes...We are planning on hiking too and we could also use some extra food.
selected_important_turn.why_important: The opponent voluntarily shared personal need for extra food (prosocial information) — a clear opening for integrative tradeoffs. Your reply immediately claimed the same scarce good, shifting the sequence from cooperative information-sharing to competition over food. That shift raised zero-sum tension at an early stage and closed off simpler cooperative solutions.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Your turn is consequential because it set the tone after the opponent's willingness to cooperate. It is improvable: rather than escalating competition by claiming the same top item, you could have reciprocated the prosocial move (asked questions, revealed secondary priorities, or proposed trades) to create mutual gains. As-is it needlessly increases conflict and reduces chances of a better joint outcome.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Reciprocate the opponent's prosocial information: acknowledge their need, reveal flexible priorities or constraints (e.g., willingness to trade food for water or firewood), or ask a clarifying question about how urgent their food need is. Offer or invite package options instead of claiming the same single item.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: would increase — opens integrative trade possibilities and allows mutually beneficial splits
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: would increase — encourages transparent tradeoffs rather than winner-take-most competition
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: would increase — creates options that make agreement simpler and quicker
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: would improve — reciprocation of cooperative tone builds trust and goodwill

value2reason.Low: we hike along a river we have plenty of water
value2reason.Medium: need to keep the kids warm at night
value2reason.High: hiking with a bunch of kids need plenty of food

value2reason.Low: We may need extra water during the day when we hike.
value2reason.Medium: It will be cold at night and we could use some extra firewood.
value2reason.High: Half of the group will be hiking and we need extra food, while the other half will be staying behind.

dialogue.text: hi how are you are you ready for your trip
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm ready, I just would like to have a little extra stuff. How about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: yes I could use some extra food since i am hiking with my kids and they all have a friend that comes along...lol
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I know how that goes...We are planning on hiking too and we could also use some extra food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Since we both need food, how about I give you 2 Food packages and you give me 2 Firewood packages? That way we both stay warm and have enough to eat.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate you sharing your need for food. Since we both value food highly, what if I take 3 food packages and you take 2, and we split the firewood and water equally? That way we both get what we need without conflict.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think splitting food 3-2 is fair, but I’d prefer to keep more firewood for the kids. How about I take 3 food, 2 firewood, and you take 1 firewood and all the water? That way we both have enough to eat and stay warm.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your need for food, but I'm concerned about the water split. Since I value food most, maybe we could trade 1 food for 1 water? That way we both get what we need without losing too much.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your point about the water trade. Let's finalize: I take 3 food, 2 firewood, and you take 1 firewood and all 3 water. That way we both meet our needs, and I can keep the kids warm.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think that works for me. Let me just confirm: you take 1 firewood and all 3 water, and I take 3 food and 2 firewood. That splits everything fairly based on our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Agreed! Final split: I take 3 Food, 2 Firewood, and you take 1 Firewood and all 3 Water. Let's head out and keep the kids warm and well-fed!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
